rulings.Case Title: IAIN BAKER VS. CHARTER COMMUNICATIONS HOLDING CO., LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 12, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANTS AECOM ENVIRONMENTAL GROUP, INC, NICOLE TESTA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied. While defendant successfully shifted the burden on each of the elements for both the negligence and motor vehicle negligence causes of action, plaintiff has presented triable issues of fact on each of the elements. Plaintiff's objection to the admissibility of the Nevada Highway Patrol accident report is overruled. Defendant's objection to plaitniff's expert declaration of Michael J. Mahoney is overruled. Both parties' request for attorney fees is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 12, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 7th and 8th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead the terms of the contract or attach a copy of the contract to the complaint. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the remaining causes of action is overruled. A party cannot demurrer to part of a cause of action and plaintiff pleads conduct and communications that are outside the Workers Compensation process. Vacanti, M.D. v. SCIF , (2001) 24 Cal,4th 800. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 12, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Special Motion to Strike (Anti-SLAPP) the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 6th, 9th and 10th causes of action is denied. Defendant has successfully established that these causes of action in the complaint contain allegations of ¿nonincidental protected activity as well as nonprotected activity and, therefore, implicates CCP 425.16. Haight Ashburry Free Clinic Inc. v. Happening House Ventures, (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539. However, Plaintiff can show a likelihood of prevailing. First, litigation privilege does not bar the entire action. Plaintiff has shown that Defendant¿s statements were not reasonably related to litigation. Defendant¿s definition of ¿litigation¿ as all of the 400 plus worker¿s compensation claims is unduly overbroad. Second, Defendant fails to show how the statement of Alcorn to get a new Doctor or the statements to Dr. Dokhanchifar to stay way form Plaintiff, not refer patients to him, and that Plaintiff was a ¿crook¿ are related to litigation or achieved an objective of litigation. See Nguyen v. Proton Technology Corp., (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 140. Moreover, Ins. Code §1877.5 does not immunize Defendant. That statute has a good faith requirement and Defendant fails to show that these communications were part of an investigation. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 12, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND MARKETING, INC'S Motion To Compel. Plaintiff's motion to compel further deposition testimony is granted as to question 1, plaintiff questioning Yeh as to what documents he reviewed to prepare for the deposition is proper. Granted as to questions 2-4 and 21-23. These questions are relevant to issues in this case including defendants' affirmative defenses of illegality and uncleand hand. Granted as to questions 8-12. The subject matter of these communications was waived and placed directly at issue by Mr. Yeh's emails. Defendant's request for reconsideration is denied. Because the witnesses here are designated as the P.M.K. for defendant, the Toyota Mortor Corp. case is clearly distinguishable. Moreover, defendant has not properly raised this issue. Requests of both parties for sanctions is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN M THOMPSON VS. BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Dawn Thompson, Anthony Jao, Aj'S Hardwood Flooring, Inc Notice Of Hearing On Motion For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 12, 2011, line 11. Plaintiff Dawn Thompson, Anthony Jao, AJ'S Hardwood Flooring, Inc's Motion For New Trial. A hearing is required. Court's tentative ruling is the same as posted August 10, 2011. - Plaintiff's motion for a new trial is granted. First, plaintiff's motion is not procedurally improper. A motion for a new trial standard does not mirror the reconsideration standard Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 859, fn.29. Plaintiff cites error of law as the basis for this motion and defendant has responded on the merits. Plaintiff cites new cases for the court to consider, Houser v. Gilbert (N.D. 1986) 389 N.W.2d 626, and Estate of Pennington v. Wolfe (D.Kan 2003) 262 F.Supp2d 1254. The Court finds these cases persuasive in supporting plaintiff's position that the failure to remove an obstruction from the roadway is independent from the operation of the vehicle and that the Court should find coverage. Accordingly, the motion for a new trial is granted based on error of law per C.C.P. 657 (7). =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PHUONG NGUYEN DDS VS. TYRONE BARBER CONSTRUCTION INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement On Behalf Of Tyrone Barber Construction Per Ccp Section 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 12, 2011, line 6. DEFENDANT TYRONE BARBER CONSTRUCTION, SUED AS TYRON BARBERT'S Motion To Enforce Settlement On Behalf Of Tyrone Barber Construction Per C.C.P. Section 664.6. Defendant's motion to enforce the settlement agreement is granted. Plaintiff is directed to authorize her attorney to release $15,600 from the client trust account to defendant's counsel's trust account. Plaintiff's former counsel is directed to remit the $15,600 to defendant's counsel's trust account. Plaintff shall also dismiss her case against defendant with prejudice. Defendant's request for attorney fees is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MAGNOLIA MARTINEZ VS. JAVA ON OCEAN COFFEE HOUSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn In Support Of Pltf'S Mtn To Quash The Subpoenas To Izzy'S Steaks & Chops, Jack'S Restaurant And Bar And California Emplotment Development Department
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 12, 2011, line 3. PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIA MARTINEZ'S Motion To Quash The Subpoenas To Izzy's Steaks & Chops, Jack's Restaurant And Bar And California Employment Development Department. Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoena is granted as to the California Employment Development Department. Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoenas as to Izzy's Steak & Chops and Jack's Restaurant is denied. However, the request is limited to records from the last three years. These are relevant to plaintiff's business reputation which is at issue in this case. =(302/LMG)
